experiments.paper_title: Ready to Translate, Not to Represent? Bias and Performance Gaps in Multilingual LLMs Across Language Families and Domains
datasets: high-quality, bias-annotated dataset (1,439 translation-reference pairs with human evaluations)
experiments.key_results: Benchmarked 24 bidirectional language pairs across multiple domains using a hybrid bias detection pipeline (rule-based heuristics, semantic similarity filtering, LLM-based validation). Findings indicate LLMs exhibit uneven performance and can encode/amplify biases, especially in low-resource languages.

experiments.paper_title: Guardians of Discourse: Evaluating LLMs on Multilingual Offensive Language Detection
datasets: Augmented translation data (used for non-English contexts)
experiments.key_results: Evaluated multilingual offensive language detection of LLMs in English, Spanish, and German, in both monolingual and multilingual settings. Examined the impact of different prompt languages and augmented translation data, and discussed the role of inherent bias in LLMs and datasets in mispredictions.

experiments.paper_title: RuBia: A Russian Language Bias Detection Dataset
datasets: RuBia (Russian language bias detection dataset, ~2,000 unique sentence pairs across 4 domains/19 subdomains)
experiments.key_results: Conducted a diagnostic evaluation of LLMs using the newly introduced RuBia dataset to illustrate the models’ predisposition to social biases, specifically in the Russian language across gender, nationality, socio-economic status, and diverse domains.

experiments.paper_title: Investigating Language and Retrieval Bias in Multilingual Previously Fact-Checked Claim Detection
metrics: Frequency of retrieved claims (for retrieval bias)
experiments.key_results: Evaluated six open-source multilingual LLMs across 20 languages for Previously Fact-Checked Claim Detection (PFCD) using a fully multilingual prompting strategy. Uncovered disparities in monolingual and cross-lingual performance due to persistent language bias. Investigated retrieval bias, revealing disproportionate retrieval of certain claims leading to inflated performance for popular claims.

experiments.paper_title: Bias in, Bias out: Annotation Bias in Multilingual Large Language Models
experiments.key_results: Proposed a comprehensive framework for understanding annotation bias (instruction, annotator, contextual/cultural bias) in NLP datasets for multilingual LLMs. Synthesized detection methods including inter-annotator agreement, model disagreement, metadata analysis, multilingual model divergence, and cultural inference, and outlined proactive/reactive mitigation strategies.
